MOTION / Notice Of Motion & Joint Motion To Set Aside Reject Voluntary Dismissal Without Prejudice By Pltfs And For Hearing On Motions For Summary Judgment/Adjudication
Off Calendar Aug-28-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES: Atty. TOM PAOLI, ESQ. of Paoli Geerhart LLP (Tel: 415-498-2101) appearing for the plaintiffs;
Atty. TODD ROBERTS, Esq. of Ropers, Majeski, Kohn & Bentley (Tel: 650-364-8200) appearing for defendant/cross-complainant State Farm Mutual Automobile Insurance Company;
Atty. KEVIN N. HEFFERNAN, ESQ. of Friedenthal Hieffernan & Brown (Tel: 626-628-2800) appearing for defendants/cross-complainants Agero Administrative Services Corp., and Cross Country Motor Club of California, Inc.;
Atty. CHARLES HORN, Esq. of Freeman Mathis & Gary LLP (Tel: 415-238-4482) for defendants/cross-defendants Irvine Auto Towing Inc., Andi Yaco, Noel Yaqo, Aram Yaco, and Jesse Coleman.

Hearing Re: Defendants and Cross-Complainants State Farm Mutual Automobile Insurance Company, Agero Administrative Services Corp., and Cross Country Motor Club of California, Inc.'s Joint Motion to Set Aside/Reject Voluntary Dismissal Without Prejudice by Plaintiffs and for Hearing on Defendants' Motions for Summary Judgment/Adjudication.

Matter argued and submitted. The Court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: Defendants and Cross-Complainants State Farm Mutual Automobile Insurance Company, Agero Administrative Services Corp., and Cross Country Motor Club of California, Inc.'s joint motion to set aside/reject voluntary dismissal without prejudice by plaintiffs and for hearing on defendants' motions for summary judgment/adjudication is denied. (See Word minutes for complete ruling.)

Order signed in open court.

The Court indicates it will vacate the hearings previously set in Dept. 302 for September 13, 2019.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman; Clerk: Sean Kane; Court Reporter: Maria A. Torreano, CSR#8600, e-mail: maria.torreano@gmail.com (302/EPS)

Notice Of Motion For Leave To File First Amended Complaint
Off Calendar Aug-06-2019 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Chuck Geerhart, Esq. of Paoli Geerhart LLP, Tel: 415.498.2101, for plaintiff and moving party;
Elaine Tanudjaja, Esq. of Friedenthal Heffernan & Brown, Tel: 626.204.2640, for defendants and opposing party;
Todd A. Roberts, Esq. of Ropers Majeski Kohn & Bentley, Tel:650.364.8200, for defendants and opposing party.

RULING: The matter was argued. At the conclusion of arguments, the Court adopted the tentative ruling on PLAINTIFFS JOSE PRECIADO, MARIA PRECIADO, AND ALINA PICAZO'S Motion For Leave To File First Amended Complaint as follows:

Plaintiffs Jose and Maria Preciado and Alina Picazo by her Guardian Ad Litem Jose Preciado's motion for leave to file first amended complaint is denied. Plaintiffs did not file this motion until after Defendants had filed their pending motions for summary judgment and the Court had granted Plaintiffs' motion for trial preference and advanced the trial date to October 15, 2019. Further, Plaintiffs admit that the reason for their motion is to attempt to meet Defendants' arguments in their motions for summary judgment, which were filed in late June and early July. Plaintiffs unduly delayed seeking leave to amend, and a belated amendment likely would prejudice Defendants. Accordingly, the motion must be denied. (See Falcon v. Long Beach Genetics, Inc. (2014) 224 Cal.App.4th 1263, 1280 ["when a plaintiff seeks leave to amend his or her complaint only after the defendant has mounted a summary judgment motion directed at the allegations of the unamended complaint, even though the plaintiff has been aware of the facts upon which the amendment is based, '[i]t would be patently unfair to allow plaintiffs to defeat [the] summary judgment motion by allowing them to present a "moving target" unbounded by the pleadings.'"]; Melican v. Regents of University of California (2007) 151 Cal.App.4th 168, 176.)

Order signed in open court.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Court Reporter: Maria A. Torreano CSR#8600, email: maria.torreano@gmail.com
